user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate 3-phase PWM motor control to new AURIX target
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate six complementary, center-aligned PWM outputs at 20 kHz on eGTM ATOM0 (Cluster 0) with 1 µs rising and falling dead-time, initial duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75%, update duties by +10% with wrap at 100%, enable syncStart and syncUpdate with zero phase shift, period interrupt toggles LED P03.9, Cpu0_Main updates every 500 ms.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified high-level IfxEgtm_Pwm on ATOM0 with DTM for complementary PWM and dead-time. Configure eGTM CMU FXCLK0 for ATOM and cmuClock0 for DTM, enable module clocks, map requested pins via IfxEgtm_PinMap in the driver config, set push-pull and cmosAutomotiveSpeed1 pads, enable syncStart/syncUpdate with center alignment and phases=0. Use the driver's interrupt configuration (mode pulseNotify on period) to toggle LED in ISR. Provide initEgtmAtom3phInv() for initialization and updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty() to step duties.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM driver supports eGTM ATOM complementary outputs with hardware dead-time, center alignment, synchronous start/update, frequency and duty updates, and integrated interrupt handling—matching all requirements and simplifying migration from IfxGtm_Pwm.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Apply +10% duty steps per phase with wrap at 100% (for U, V, W) while running at 20 kHz, maintaining complementary, center-aligned outputs with configured dead-time and synchronous update.

api_changes.reason: TC3xx→TC4xx watchdog migration; use IfxWtu APIs available in template

migration_notes.context_preserved: All original requirements are mapped to IfxEgtm_Pwm: ATOM0 on Cluster 0 with complementary center-aligned PWM at 20 kHz, 1 µs rise/fall dead-time via DTM, syncStart/syncUpdate enabled with zero phases, initial duties U/V/W=25/50/75%, 10% step per 500 ms update, and a period-event interrupt (pulseNotify) toggling LED P03.9. FXCLK0 is used for ATOM and cmuClock0 for DTM via IfxEgtm_Cmu_enableClocks, and outputs are configured as push-pull with cmosAutomotiveSpeed1 pad driver via the PinMap in the driver config. Channel order default (CH0→V, CH1→U, CH2→W) is used as requested. Note: The provided TOUT pin pairs must be validated against IfxEgtm_PinMap for TC4D7 to ensure complementary pairing on the same ATOM channel and CDTM; adjust to available macros if needed.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
